title: Photosynthesis Spectral Analysis Lab
description: A chemistry lab workbench where a bioluminescent plant specimen sits under an iridescent UV analysis lamp. The user must calibrate the lamp's wavelength, intensity, and position to stimulate different photosynthetic responses in the plant, then record spectral absorption readings across multiple frequency bands to complete an analysis report.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements an interactive photosynthesis spectral analysis lab. Load DiffuseTransmissionPlant.glb as a plant specimen placed on a lab bench surface (a simple box geometry with a dark matte material) at world center. Load IridescenceLamp.glb as an adjustable analysis lamp mounted above the plant, initially positioned at (0, 2.5, 0). Use GLTFLoader to load both assets. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at (3, 3, 4) looking at the origin with OrbitControls for navigation. Add subtle ambient light (intensity 0.3) and a dim directional light simulating room lighting. The interaction sequence requires these steps: (1) Click a 'Power On' HTML button to activate the lamp, triggering a SpotLight child on the lamp model pointing at the plant with visible cone via SpotLightHelper. (2) Use an HTML range slider labeled 'Wavelength (380-780nm)' that changes the SpotLight color mapping across the visible spectrum using Color.setHSL. (3) Use a second slider 'Intensity (0-100%)' controlling SpotLight intensity from 0 to 5. (4) Click and drag the lamp model horizontally along X-axis (raycaster-based drag) to reposition it over different parts of the plant. (5) Adjust lamp height with a 'Height' slider moving Y position between 1.5 and 4.0. (6) Click a 'Measure Absorption' button that reads current wavelength, intensity, and position values, validates that intensity is above 40% and lamp height is below 3.0, then displays a computed absorption percentage in an overlay panel. (7) Repeat measurement at three different wavelength bands (red 620-780, green 495-570, blue 380-495) tracked by the system. (8) Once all three bands are recorded with valid readings, a 'Submit Report' button enables, and clicking it shows a summary panel with all readings and a completion message. The plant material should react to the lamp: apply a custom ShaderMaterial or modify the plant's material emissive property to glow faintly in the color of the current wavelength when intensity exceeds 50%. Add a translucent green grid overlay on the bench for a scientific aesthetic. All UI controls should be positioned as an HTML panel on the left side with clean styling.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  lampPowered: boolean,         // whether the UV analysis lamp is currently powered on
  lampWavelength: number,         // current wavelength setting in nanometers (380-780)
  lampIntensity: number,         // current light intensity percentage (0-100)
  lampPositionX: number,         // horizontal X position of the lamp in world space
  lampPositionY: number,         // vertical Y position of the lamp in world space
  plantIlluminated: boolean,         // whether the plant is receiving sufficient light exposure
  spectralReadings: number,         // count of successful spectral measurements recorded
  absorptionRate: number,         // calculated photosynthetic absorption rate (0-1)
  calibrationComplete: boolean,         // whether lamp calibration meets analysis requirements
  dragActive: boolean,         // whether user is currently dragging the lamp
}
